generated_oml:
instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent‑magnet synchronous motor current sensor providing time‑series current data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MQTTPayload
]

instance OutputAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear output angle sensor providing time‑series angular position data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MQTTPayload
]

instance MQTTPayload : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor, OutputAngleSensor
]

instance CurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series current measurement"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBroker
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTPayload
]

instance AngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series output angle measurement"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBroker
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTPayload
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker handling real‑time streaming of robot sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Matlab/Simulink execution environment for closed‑loop simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulinkSimulationService
]

instance SysMLArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 functional and logical architecture model of MABI MAX 100 robot"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance SimulinkSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulink service executing dynamic models derived from SysML architecture"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationOutcome
]

instance FrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Friction characteristics derived from aggregated sensor traces"
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of Siemens SINUMERIK PLC control parameters to improve precision"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SimulationOutcome : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Outcome of the closed‑loop simulation run in Simulink"
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating context data traces to compute friction models for control optimization"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionInsight, ControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance PMMSMotor : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Permanent‑magnet synchronous motor powering the MABI MAX 100 robotic arm"
]

instance CycloidalGearTransmission : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear transmission system directly connected to the robot joints"
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D PLC controlling the robot’s motion execution"
]

instance RobotMABI : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "6‑DOF MABI MAX 100 industrial robot arm operating in a precision manufacturing environment"
    base:contains PMMSMotor, CycloidalGearTransmission, SiemensSINUMERIK
]

instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RobotMABI
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "Modeling in a digital twin follows MBSE practices that separate the system description into several abstraction layers: requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layer. These layers represent successive stages in the twin’s lifecycle, with higher‑level models informing the development of more concrete ones. Transition between stages relies on maintaining traceability across the different abstraction layers. The article does not specify explicit governance artifacts for life‑cycle management."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The MQTT broker operates over TCP/IP on the standard port 1883 (or 8883 for TLS), with QoS level 0 or 1. The network topology follows a publish/subscribe architecture: each sensor acts as a publisher, and the ingestion layer subscribes to relevant topics. No additional transport protocols or encryption layers are specified in the document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The text references the use of standardized architecture description languages: SysML (including IBDs), ACME, AADL, and MontiArc. These are recognized modeling standards for system specification. No other industry‑specific or certification standards are mentioned in the provided document."
]
